evals.prompt: Design a pavement structure for a Class I trunk road in Uganda using the MoWT catalogue method. The design traffic is 4.5 million ESAs over a 15-year design life. The design CBR is 6% (10th percentile from 12 DCP tests along the route). The road is in a region with annual rainfall of 450 mm, good drainage provision and well-maintained drainage structures. Using the MoWT Road Design Manual Vol 3 Part I, determine the traffic class, subgrade class, wet/dry condition, select the appropriate chart type, and provide the pavement structure from the catalogue.
evals.expected_output: Traffic class T5 (3-6M ESAs). Subgrade class S3 (CBR 5-7%). Table 4.1 wet/dry check: rainfall 450mm with good drainage and maintenance = D (Dry). Chart selection per Table 5.3: for T5 traffic, D3 (cemented base/cemented subbase) or D4 (bituminous base/granular subbase) appropriate. Structure from Appendix C for selected chart and T5/S3 combination, with layer materials and thicknesses.

assertions.text: Correctly classifies 4.5M ESAs as traffic class T5 (range 3-6M)
Correctly classifies CBR 6% as subgrade class S3 (range 5-7%)
Performs Table 4.1 wet/dry check BEFORE chart type selection -- determines D (dry) condition
Selects chart type using Table 5.3 classification -- justifies D3, D4, or D5 selection for T5 traffic
Provides specific layer thicknesses from catalogue (base 100-225 mm range, subbase 150-225 mm range depending on chart)

evals.prompt: Design a pavement for a road in the UK with 20 million ESAs design traffic and a subgrade with CBR of 8%. Use the MoWT catalogue method to select a pavement structure.
evals.expected_output: Notes that MoWT catalogue method is not UK practice. References CD 227 as the applicable UK standard. Describes UK foundation class approach (Foundation Class 3 for CBR 8%). Flags that no local CD 227 document is available for exact values.

assertions.text: Identifies that MoWT catalogue is not UK practice and references CD 227 as the UK equivalent
Describes UK foundation class concept (not CBR-based subgrade classes)
Flags that no local CD 227 document is available for exact design values

evals.prompt: Design a pavement using the MoWT catalogue for a road in Uganda with 2.8 million ESAs (near the T4/T5 boundary at 3 million), CBR 4% (subgrade class S2), in a region with annual rainfall 1200mm, poor side drainage (ditches frequently blocked), and uncertain maintenance. Determine the appropriate pavement structure and flag any design concerns.
evals.expected_output: Traffic class T4 (1.5-3M ESAs) but ESA within 15% of T4/T5 boundary (2.8M vs 3M) -- sensitivity flag raised, both T4 and T5 structures should be provided. Subgrade class S2 (CBR 3-4%). Table 4.1 wet/dry: high rainfall (1200mm) + poor drainage + deficient maintenance = W (Wet) condition. W-chart structures selected. Escalation for poor subgrade (S2 may require capping). Sensitivity analysis for T4 vs T5.

assertions.text: Raises sensitivity flag for ESA 2.8M being within 15% of T4/T5 boundary at 3M ESAs
Determines W (Wet) condition from Table 4.1 due to 1200mm rainfall, poor drainage, and deficient maintenance
Selects W-chart (not D-chart) for the pavement structure
Flags poor subgrade S2 (CBR 4%) requiring capping layer consideration
Provides structures for both T4 and T5 due to boundary sensitivity

evals.prompt: Design a flexible pavement for a Kenya Class A road (KeNHA) with cumulative traffic of 12 MCESA on a subgrade with design CBR of 8%. The project is in a wet climate zone near Lake Victoria.
evals.expected_output: Uses Kenya RDM 3.4 mechanistic-empirical method (not Uganda MoWT). Traffic class TLC07 (10-17 MESA). Kenya subgrade class for CBR 8%. Selects from 16 standard structure types -- bituminous base structure (ST07 or ST09) appropriate for TLC07 in wet zone. Uses MESA/CESA terminology throughout. References mechanistic-empirical basis of RDM 3.4.

assertions.text: Uses Kenya RDM 3.4 as the design standard (not Uganda MoWT catalogue)
Selects from the 16 standard structure types (ST01-ST16), not Uganda D/W charts
Uses MESA or CESA terminology throughout (not plain ESA without prefix)
References Kenya subgrade classes from RDM 3.3 (not Uganda S1-S6 with MoWT boundaries)
Mentions the mechanistic-empirical basis of RDM 3.4 (MLET, strain calculations, or transfer functions)

evals.prompt: Design a flexible pavement for an ERA trunk road in Ethiopia using the ERA design catalogue method. The design traffic is 8 million ESAs over a 20-year design period. The subgrade has a design CBR of 10% (10th percentile). Using the ERA Flexible Pavement Design Manual Vol I 2013 Chapter 10, determine the traffic class, subgrade class, select the appropriate design chart, and provide the pavement structure.
evals.expected_output: Traffic class T6 (6-10M ESA) per ERA classification. Subgrade class S3 (CBR 8-14%) per ERA Table 3-1. Chart selection from ERA catalogue groups A1-D based on surfacing type and base type. Pavement structure from the selected chart for T6/S3 combination. Damage exponent n=4.5. ERA uses 7 chart groups (A1-A3, B, C1-C2, D), not Uganda D1-D5/W1-W5 wet/dry charts or Kenya ST01-ST16 structure types.

assertions.text: References ERA Flexible Pavement Design Manual Vol I 2013 Chapter 10 (not Uganda MoWT or Kenya RDM 3.4)
Classifies 8M ESAs as traffic class T6 (6-10M ESA range)
Classifies CBR 10% as subgrade class S3 (8-14%) using ERA's five-class system
Selects from ERA chart groups A1-D (not Uganda D/W charts or Kenya ST structure types)
Uses ERA design period of 20 years for trunk road and damage exponent n=4.5

evals.prompt: A consulting engineer is designing pavement structures for road projects in both Ethiopia and Uganda. Both projects have the same traffic loading (3 million cumulative ESAs) and subgrade conditions (CBR 5%). Compare the ERA pavement design catalogue approach with the Uganda MoWT catalogue approach. What pavement structures would each standard recommend? Highlight key differences in layer thicknesses, material specifications, and design methodology.
evals.expected_output: ERA catalogue: references ERA Pavement Design Manual flexible pavement catalogue for 3M ESAs on CBR 5% subgrade. MoWT catalogue: references Uganda MoWT Road Design Manual pavement catalogue for equivalent traffic class and subgrade. Comparison of recommended layer thicknesses (surface, base, subbase), material specifications, and any differences in design philosophy. Notes ERA's relationship to TRL ORN 31 methodology.

assertions.text: References ERA Pavement Design Manual for the Ethiopian design
References Uganda MoWT Road Design Manual for the Ugandan design
Compares specific layer thicknesses from each catalogue
Does not apply Kenya RDM to either design unless explicitly comparing all three
Notes differences in design methodology or material specifications between ERA and MoWT
